revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:伊賀津臣命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Nashitomi|qid=Q108616765|1=Nashitomi}}; {{ill|Q110796281|qid=Q110796281|1=Omotomochi no Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Mishokutsuomi no Mikoto|qid=Q110796235|1=Mishokutsuomi no Mikoto}}}}
{{Infobox noble | name = Ikatsuno Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Yayoi period]], ancient Japan | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ikatsuno Mikoto, Ikatsutomi, Ikatomi | honorary_title = | deity_name = | posthumous_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagu Shrine|1=Ikagu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381016}} | Rank = | lord = | clan = {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|qid=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Miketsuno Mikoto|ja|御食津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miketsuno Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505720|1=Mishokutsuomi no Mikoto}} | siblings = | spouse = {{ill|Celestial maiden|ja|天女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial maiden|qq=}}<ref>Excerpts from *Omi no Kuni Fudoki*</ref> | children = {{ill|Nashitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi-no-Mikoto|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108616765}}, Omichito-no-Mikoto, Izerihime, Nazerihime | notable_events = }} '''Ikatsuno Mikoto''' (伊賀津臣命, {{ill|生没年不詳|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/生没年不詳|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was an ancient Japanese noble regarded as the founder of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中臣氏}}. In the *{{ill|Omi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi no Kuni Fudoki|1=Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638480}}* excerpts, he is referred to as '''Ikatsutomi''' or '''Ikatomi''', while one version of the *{{ill|Sonpi Bunmyaku|en|Sonpi Bunmyaku|qid=Q1704738|lt=Sonpi Bunmyaku|1=Sonpi Bunmyaku}}* refers to him as '''Ikatsuno Mikoto.'''
== Overview ==
Although he does not appear in the *{{ill|Records of Ancient Matters|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Ancient Matters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kiki (Shinto)|qid=Q842308}}* and little is known about his accomplishments, Ikatsuno Mikoto features in the legend of the *{{ill|Hagoromo|en|Hagoromo|qid=Q1208551|lt=Hagoromo|1=Hagoromo}}* (Celestial Robe of Feathers) recounted in the *{{ill|Omi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi no Kuni Fudoki|1=Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638480}}*. The text describes him as a resident of the mountains in the {{ill|Yogo District|ja|与胡郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yogo District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}. According to the story, Ikatsutomi spied on celestial maidens (*Amanohachime*, daughters of heaven) bathing with a swan in a nearby lake known as {{ill|Ikakoe|ja|伊香小江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikakoe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and fell in love at first sight. He sent a white dog to stealthily steal the celestial robe (*Hagoromo*) of the youngest maiden, thereby preventing her return to heaven. Ikatsutomi then married the celestial maiden and had four children: Omichito, {{ill|Nashitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi-no-Mikoto|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108616765}}, Izerihime, and Nazerihime. The youngest celestial maiden eventually retrieved her robe and ascended back to heaven, leaving Ikatsutomi to live a lonely life.
Ikatsuno Mikoto is venerated as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Ikagu Shrine|ja|伊香具神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagu Shrine|1=Ikagu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381016}}. According to shrine traditions, he arrived during ancient times to reclaim undeveloped lands in the area and later protected his descendants.<ref>{{Cite web |title=History and Origins – Ikagu Shrine |url=https://ikagu-jinjya.com/history/ |access-date=2024-04-30 |language=ja}}</ref>
== Genealogy ==
Ikatsuno Mikoto is described as the son of {{ill|Miketsuno Mikoto|ja|御食津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miketsuno Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505720|1=Mishokutsuomi no Mikoto}} and the husband of the youngest celestial maiden (*Tennyo*). According to the *{{ill|Omi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|近江国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi no Kuni Fudoki|1=Ōmi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638480}}* excerpts, his children are identified as Omichito (Omichito-no-Mikoto, later known as *臣知人命*<ref>*{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}*, Vol. 11, Sakyo Kamibetsu-jin Section, Nakatomi lineage.</ref>), {{ill|Nashitomi-no-Mikoto|ja|梨迹臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashitomi-no-Mikoto|1=Nashitomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108616765}}, Izerihime, and Nazerihime.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also == * {{ill|Radical 25|en|Radical 25|qid=Q94724|lt=Radical 25 (卜)|1=Radical 25}} * {{ill|Urame clan|ja|占部氏|1=Urabe clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urame clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 卜部氏}} * {{ill|List of Pre-Asuka Period Figures|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Pre-Asuka Period Figures|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665973}}
{{translated page|ja|伊賀津臣命|version=101444680|comment=Imported full ja history}}
